name: Real-time / safety-critical control system
summary: A hard real-time system that closes a control loop over physical actuators under strict timing, where a missed deadline or wrong output can cause harm. Value and acceptance are governed by functional-safety standards (SIL/ASIL/DAL), provable worst-case timing, and certified fail-safe behaviour.

concerns: hazard analysis and risk assessment (HARA/PHA/FMEA/FTA) deriving safety goals and integrity levels
assigned safety integrity level (SIL per IEC 61508, ASIL per ISO 26262, DAL per DO-178C) and the process rigour it mandates
fail-safe / safe-state definition and the fault-reaction / fault-tolerant time interval to reach it
degraded / limp-home operating modes and controlled transitions between them
diagnostic coverage, single-point-fault and latent-fault metrics for detecting dangerous failures
freedom from interference between mixed-criticality functions (partitioning)
questions: Has a hazard analysis derived safety goals with an assigned SIL/ASIL/DAL?
Is a safe state defined, with a fault-reaction/fault-tolerant time interval to reach it?
Are degraded modes, diagnostic-coverage targets, and freedom-from-interference specified?

concerns: hard deadlines: worst-case execution time (WCET) bounded and proven, not measured-typical
deterministic timing with bounded jitter on control-loop period and end-to-end sensor-to-actuator latency
schedulability analysis (RMA/response-time analysis) proving all tasks meet deadlines under worst case
bounded interrupt latency and elimination of unbounded blocking (priority inversion control)
avoidance of nondeterministic constructs (dynamic allocation, unbounded loops, cache-induced variance)
questions: Is worst-case execution time bounded and proven for all real-time tasks (not just measured typical)?
Is a schedulability analysis required proving every deadline is met under worst-case conditions?
Are control-loop period, jitter, and sensor-to-actuator latency limits specified deterministically?

concerns: redundancy architecture (dual/triple modular redundancy, hot/cold standby) and voting
fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) and failover timing within the fault-tolerant interval
target failure measures: PFH/PFD per SIL, hardware-fault-tolerance, and MTBF/MTTF budgets
common-cause failure analysis and independence/diversity of redundant channels
graceful, deterministic degradation rather than abrupt failure
questions: Is a redundancy/voting architecture with defined failover timing specified?
Are quantitative failure targets (PFH/PFD per SIL, HFT, MTBF) stated?
Is common-cause failure between redundant channels analysed and mitigated?

concerns: correctness of the control law / state machine and its stability margins under all operating conditions
complete specification of exception, boundary, and fault conditions (no undefined behaviour)
formal verification / model checking of safety-critical logic where mandated by the integrity level
deterministic, repeatable behaviour for identical inputs and state
range/plausibility checking of sensor inputs and actuator command limits
questions: Is the control law's correctness and stability specified across the full operating envelope?
Are all exception and fault conditions fully specified with no undefined behaviour?
Is formal verification of safety-critical logic required at the assigned integrity level?

concerns: bidirectional requirements traceability from hazard to requirement to design to code to test (as certification evidence)
structural test coverage mandated by integrity level (e.g. MC/DC for DAL A / ASIL D)
coding-standard conformance (MISRA C/C++) and defensive-coding rules; qualified static analysis
verification of timing and safety properties, not only functional behaviour
change impact analysis and configuration management to preserve certification on change
questions: Is full bidirectional traceability (hazard-requirement-design-code-test) required as certification evidence?
Is the mandated structural coverage (e.g. MC/DC) and coding-standard conformance specified?
Is change-impact analysis required to preserve certification when code changes?

concerns: deterministic scheduling policy (fixed-priority preemptive, time-triggered / TT architecture) and its configuration
operation and safe transitions across power-up, run, degraded, maintenance, and shutdown modes
startup self-tests / built-in test (POST/BIT) and periodic online diagnostics
safe update/maintenance procedures without compromising the safety function in the field
operator warnings, alarms, and manual-override / emergency-stop handling
questions: Is a deterministic scheduling policy specified with its task priorities/time-triggered schedule?
Are startup and periodic self-tests (BIT) and safe mode transitions defined?
Are operator warnings, manual override, and emergency-stop behaviour specified?

concerns: mandated certification standard and independent assessment (IEC 61508 / ISO 26262 / DO-178C / IEC 62304) as a hard deliverable
qualified/certified toolchain, compiler, and RTOS (tool qualification, pre-certified components / SEooC)
prohibition of nondeterministic language features and mandated safe language subset
certification timeline, assessor sign-off, and evidence/artifact obligations
target hardware and its safety-qualified components (lockstep cores, ECC memory)
questions: Is the mandated functional-safety certification standard and independent assessment captured as a constraint?
Are a qualified/certified toolchain, RTOS, and pre-certified components required?
Are the safety-qualified target hardware and the certification-evidence obligations specified?

concerns: deterministic communication buses and their timing (CAN/CAN-FD, FlexRay, ARINC 429/664, TSN, EtherCAT, PROFINET)
safety communication protocols with end-to-end protection (e.g. black-channel, CRC, sequence counters)
time synchronisation across distributed nodes (PTP/IEEE 1588, TTEthernet)
interface versioning and compatibility across mixed-criticality integrated systems
questions: Are the deterministic communication buses and their timing/protection specified?
Is time synchronisation across distributed nodes defined for the control loop?
